The classification of Chamaepeuce fruticosa is further detailed in the kingdom Plantae, and the phylum Magnoliophyta. uses: Chamaepeuce fruticosa, also known as the grass-leaved golden sedge, has been used in traditional medicine due to its various properties. parts: Chamaepeuce fruticosa, also known as the wild ginger, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. preparations: Chamaepeuce fruticosa, also known as Winter Savory, is a plant used in herbal preparations for its medicinal and culinary properties. The leaves of Winter Savory contain compounds like apigenin and luteolin, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
